L1.area: Database replication and data synchronization infrastructure

L2.description: A cryptographic weakness exists where UUID identifiers are generated using a pseudo-random number generator that is not cryptographically secure. An attacker who can observe prior UUID outputs could predict future values, allowing them to forge or guess identity tokens used in database communication protocols.

L3.area: Binary log replication synchronization and connection setup logic
L3.description: A cryptographic weakness exists where UUID identifiers used during replica connection initialization in the binary log replication protocol are generated using a pseudo-random number generator that is not cryptographically secure. An attacker who can observe prior UUID outputs could predict future values, allowing them to impersonate replica instances during the replication handshake.

ground_truth.reason: The project uses the satori/go.uuid library which employs math/rand instead of crypto/rand for UUID generation, making generated UUIDs predictable to an attacker who can observe prior outputs.
